perturbation.original_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 60 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 30 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text shortens the period during which parties are restricted from trading stock after the conclusion of transaction discussions from '60 days' to '30 days.' This directly contradicts the original clause, creating ambiguity about the actual restricted period, which is legally binding, and creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 30 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 60-day restriction period, which was changed to 30 days. The contradicted text contains the changed text with the 30-day restriction. Since the original document now contains both a 60-day and a 30-day restriction, a clear in-text contradiction exists concerning the duration of the restriction period. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as defined, because it introduces conflicting deadlines within the contract. The changed text directly conflicts with the original, creating ambiguity about the actual enforceable duration of the restriction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be modified or waived only by a separate writing by Holdings and Big Sky expressly so modifying or waiving such Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified or waived only by a separate writing by Holdings and Big Sky, or by oral agreement with confirmation in writing, expressly so modifying or waiving such Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the original requirement for modifications or waivers to be in writing. Introducing the possibility of oral agreements (with written confirmation) creates an in-text contradiction about how modifications are legally valid, potentially leading to disputes over the enforceability of oral agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be modified or waived only by a separate writing by Holdings and Big Sky, or by oral agreement with confirmation in writing, expressly so modifying or waiving such Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that modifications or waivers must be in writing. The changed text introduces the possibility of oral agreements with written confirmation. This directly contradicts the initial requirement for written modifications only. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but based on the explanation, the change from the original text creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Big Sky and Holdings hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive the right to a jury trial in connection with any claim arising out of related to this Agreement, the Information, or the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Big Sky and Holdings hereby waive the right to a jury trial in connection with any claim arising out of related to this Agreement, the Information, or the Transaction; however, this waiver may be revoked by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that allows either party to revoke the jury trial waiver with 30 days written notice. This contradicts the original statement that the waiver is 'irrevocable and unconditional', creating uncertainty about the enforceability of the waiver and whether a jury trial is truly waived.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Big Sky and Holdings hereby waive the right to a jury trial in connection with any claim arising out of related to this Agreement, the Information, or the Transaction; however, this waiver may be revoked by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by adding a clause that allows either party to revoke the jury trial waiver with 30 days written notice, directly contradicting the initial statement that the waiver is 'irrevocable and unconditional.' This creates an inconsistency, as defined by the prompt, leading to uncertainty about the enforceability of the waiver.
